content.title: Asian Stocks Shrug Off Tariff Jitters
content.summary: Asian stocks rose broadly, with South Korea and Taiwan setting records, despite renewed trade uncertainty.  Hong Kong’s Hang Seng Index rose 2.5%.   South Korea’s Kospi composite closed at a record high, as did its biggest component, Samsung Electronics.

content.title: Indian Stocks Advance After U.S. Agrees to Slash Tariffs
content.summary: Indian stocks jumped after President Trump agreed to slash tariffs on the country. The U.S. is lowering levies on Indian goods to 18% after the two nations struck a trade pact and Trump said New Delhi, led by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, agreed to stop buying Russian oil.

content.title: EU-India Trade Deal by the Numbers
content.summary: That's the annual saving equivalent the European Union expects as a result of its newly struck trade deal with India.  The EU says the pact is set to eliminate or reduce tariffs for more than 90% of its goods.  It is the latest example of U.S. trading partners seeking to curb their reliance on America by expanding ties with other markets.

content.title: Bitcoin hovers near $78K as crypto industry pushes for regulation
content.description:
Cryptocurrency bitcoin (BTC-USD) falls but manages to hold above $78,000 per token on Thursday. This comes after crypto firms pen a letter to US lawmakers, pushing for the passing of stablecoin regulation.

Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman takes a closer at the crypto market and crypto-related stocks.
content.summary: Cryptocurrency bitcoin (BTC-USD) falls but manages to hold above $78,000 per token on Thursday. This comes after crypto firms pen a letter to US lawmakers, pushing for the passing of stablecoin regulation. Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman takes a closer at the crypto market and crypto-related stocks.

content.title: Strategy (MSTR) Is Up 15.8% After Record US$2.54B Bitcoin Buy Funded By Equity Machine
content.summary: Earlier this week, Strategy Inc. disclosed its largest Bitcoin purchase to date, buying US$2.54 billion worth of the cryptocurrency to lift its holdings to 815,061 BTC and surpass BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust as the world’s biggest institutional holder. The company funded this record acquisition primarily through sales of its STRC perpetual preferred stock and new equity, highlighting an unusual capital-raising “machine” that converts investor demand directly into additional Bitcoin on...

content.title: Microsoft and Meta to cut thousands of jobs
content.summary: Microsoft and Meta, the company behind Instagram and WhatsApp, are planning to cut big chucks from their respective workforces as spending on artificial intelligence (AI) finds a new high gear.  Meta said it was laying off about 8,000 workers, or about 10% of its workforce, in a bid to make efficiency savings as it ramps up investment.  Meta had previously told investors that spending would top $160bn during 2026 - up from just shy of $120bn last year.

content.title: Oil extends gains, stocks sink as peace talk hopes fade
content.summary: Oil prices extended gains Friday and stocks struggled as investors worried about a lack of progress in ending the Middle East crisis, with Tehran keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed and the US maintaining a blockade of Iranian ports."So far, no meaningful progress has been made in negotiations, with both sides maintaining relatively firm positions, leaving the short-term outlook unclear.

thumbnail.caption: Iran said it would not join talks to reopen the Strait of Hormuz while the United States maintained a blockade of the country's ports (-)

content.description:
Tesla (TSLA) reported first quarter results on Wednesday after the closing bell. Adjusted earnings per share (EPS) came in at $0.41 (compared to analyst estimates of $0.34), and revenue came in at $22.39 billion (compared to analyst estimates of $22.19 billion).

Yahoo Finance Senior Autos Reporter Pras Subramanian and Barron's associate editor Al Root discuss what investors need from Tesla on robotaxi and robots.
